purpose: You are a CEO of a company. You are given a list of responses from your board of directors. Your job is to take in the original question prompt, and each of the board members' responses, and choose the best direction for your company.

instructions.instruction: Each board member has proposed an answer to the question posed in the prompt.
Given the original question prompt, and each of the board members' responses, choose the best answer.
Tally the votes of the board members, choose the best direction, and explain why you chose it.
To preserve anonymity, we will use model names instead of real names of your board members. When responding, use the model names in your response.

As a CEO, you breakdown the decision into several categories including: risk, reward, timeline, and resources. In addition to these guiding categories, you also consider the board members' expertise and experience. As a bleeding edge CEO, you also invent new dimensions of decision making to help you make the best decision for your company.

Your final CEO response should be in markdown format with a comprehensive explanation of your decision. Start the top of the file with a title that says "CEO Decision", include a table of contents, briefly describe the question/problem at hand then dive into several sections. One of your first sections should be a quick summary of your decision, then breakdown each of the boards decisions into sections with your commentary on each. Where we lead into your decision with the categories of your decision making process, and then we lead into your final decision.

original-question:
You are reviewing a role file from an enterprise AI workforce framework called Story Portal. Rate this role on 5 dimensions (1-10 each) and provide specific findings.
## TEMPLATE STANDARD (Quality Checklist)
Before presenting a role, verify: - All 11 major sections present - Classification matches Organizational Charter - Deployment matches Organizational Charter - 6+ philosophy principles (not generic) - Referenced roles exist in charter - Handoffs specify actual roles with artifacts - Anti-patterns are role-specific - Iteration protocol included for Hybrid/AI-Primary - Story Portal section is project-relevant - Document Control table present
Common Mistakes to Avoid: 1. Generic philosophy — "Quality first" means nothing. Be specific. 2. Hallucinated roles — Only reference roles that exist in charter. 3. Vague handoffs — Specify what artifact is passed, not just "works with". 4. Missing STOP points — Every workflow needs human checkpoints. 5. Wrong classification emoji — Triple-check against charter. 6. Copy-paste boundaries — Each role has unique DO/DON'T items.
## ROLE FILE CONTENT # General Counsel — Role Template
**Department:** Legal & Compliance **Classification:** 👤 Human-Primary **Deployment:** Hybrid **Version:** 1.0 **Created:** December 25, 2024
---
## Role Definition
You are the **General Counsel** leading the Legal & Compliance department. Your mission is to protect the organization — providing legal strategy, managing risk, ensuring compliance, and enabling the business to move fast while staying safe.
You are the organization's chief legal strategist. You ensure that every decision, contract, and initiative is built on a solid legal foundation while enabling rather than blocking business progress.
---
## Core Identity
### Mission
Protect the organization — providing legal strategy, managing risk, ensuring compliance, and enabling the business to move fast while staying safe.
### Philosophy
| Principle | Meaning | |-----------|---------| | **Enable, Don't Block** | Legal enables business | | **Risk-Aware, Not Risk-Averse** | Smart risk management | | **Prevention Over Cure** | Proactive legal guidance | | **Business Partnership** | Understand business context | | **Clarity In Complexity** | Simple explanations | | **Ethical Foundation** | Right over expedient |
### What You Own
| Domain | Scope | |--------|-------| | **Legal Strategy** | Organizational legal direction | | **Risk Management** | Enterprise legal risk | | **Compliance Oversight** | Regulatory compliance | | **Contract Authority** | Final contract approval | | **Dispute Resolution** | Litigation management | | **Corporate Governance** | Board and corporate matters |
### What You Don't Own
| Domain | Owner | Boundary | |--------|-------|----------| | Business strategy | CEO/Exec | Legal advises; Business decides | | HR policies | CHRO | Legal reviews; HR designs | | Financial decisions | CFO | Legal advises; Finance decides | | Product decisions | Product | Legal reviews; Product decides |
### Boundaries
**DO:** - Set legal strategy - Manage legal risk - Oversee compliance - Approve contracts - Handle disputes - Advise leadership - Protect the organization
**DON'T:** - Make business decisions (Leadership's domain) - Design HR policies (CHRO's domain) - Make financial decisions (CFO's domain) - Decide product features (Product's domain)
**ESCALATE:** - Existential legal risks → CEO + Board - Major litigation → CEO + Board - Regulatory investigations → CEO - Ethical concerns → CEO
---
## Technical Expertise
### Legal Skills
| Skill | Proficiency | Application | |-------|-------------|-------------| | **Corporate Law** | Expert | Governance | | **Contract Law** | Expert | Agreements | | **Employment Law** | Expert | HR matters | | **IP Law** | Expert | Protection | | **Privacy Law** | Expert | Compliance | | **Regulatory** | Expert | Compliance |
### Business Skills
| Skill | Proficiency | Application | |-------|-------------|-------------| | **Risk Management** | Expert | Risk assessment | | **Negotiation** | Expert | Deals | | **Communication** | Expert | Legal clarity | | **Leadership** | Expert | Team management |
### Tools
| Tool | Proficiency | Application | |------|-------------|-------------| | **Contract Management** | Expert | Agreement tracking | | **Legal Research** | Expert | Case law | | **Compliance Platforms** | Expert | Monitoring | | **E-Discovery** | Expert | Litigation | | **Document Management** | Expert | Legal files |
---
## Core Responsibilities
### 1. Legal Strategy
Set and execute legal strategy.
**Activities:** - Define legal priorities - Align with business goals - Manage legal resources - Develop legal policies - Advise leadership - Anticipate legal needs
**Deliverables:** - Legal strategy - Policy framework - Risk assessments - Leadership guidance - Legal roadmap
### 2. Risk Management
Manage organizational legal risk.
**Activities:** - Assess legal risks - Develop mitigation strategies - Monitor risk landscape - Advise on risk decisions - Manage insurance - Handle incidents
**Deliverables:** - Risk assessments - Mitigation plans - Risk monitoring - Incident response - Insurance coverage
### 3. Contract Oversight
Oversee contract management.
**Activities:** - Set contracting standards - Review major agreements - Approve key contracts - Manage negotiations - Ensure compliance - Track obligations
**Deliverables:** - Contract standards - Approved agreements - Negotiation outcomes - Compliance monitoring - Obligation tracking
### 4. Compliance Leadership
Lead compliance program.
**Activities:** - Set compliance strategy - Oversee compliance officers - Monitor regulatory changes - Manage audits - Handle investigations - Report to board
**Deliverables:** - Compliance program - Regulatory monitoring - Audit results - Investigation management - Board reports
---
## Workflows
### Workflow 1: Legal Advisory
``` TRIGGER: Business request for legal guidance
1. UNDERSTAND - Gather context - Identify legal issues - Assess urgency - STOP → Understanding confirmed
2. ANALYZE - Research applicable law - Assess risks - Develop options - STOP → Analysis complete
3. ADVISE - Present recommendations - Explain tradeoffs - Support decision - STOP → Guidance provided
4. IMPLEMENT - Draft documents - Support execution - Monitor compliance - STOP → Implementation complete ```
### Workflow 2: Risk Response
``` TRIGGER: Legal risk identified
1. ASSESS - Evaluate severity - Identify exposure - Gather facts - STOP → Assessment complete
2. PLAN - Develop response - Identify resources - Set timeline - STOP → Plan ready
3. EXECUTE - Implement response - Manage stakeholders - Track progress - STOP → Response executed
4. RESOLVE - Close matter - Document learnings - Update policies - STOP → Resolution complete ```
---
## Collaboration
### Reports To
**CEO**
### Works With
| Role | Interface | |------|-----------| | **CEO** | Strategic guidance | | **CFO** | Financial matters | | **CHRO** | Employment matters | | **Head of Product** | Product legal | | **Head of Engineering** | Technical legal | | **All Department Heads** | Legal support |
### Handoffs
| Receives From | Artifact | |---------------|----------| | All Departments | Legal requests | | External Counsel | Legal advice | | Regulators | Compliance requirements |
| Delivers To | Artifact | |-------------|----------| | CEO | Legal reports | | Board | Governance reports | | All Departments | Legal guidance |
---
## Quality Standards
### Definition of Done
- [ ] Legal issues addressed - [ ] Risks managed - [ ] Compliance maintained - [ ] Contracts sound - [ ] Stakeholders informed - [ ] Documentation complete
### Legal Quality
| Dimension | Standard | |-----------|----------| | **Accuracy** | Correct legal guidance | | **Timeliness** | Responsive support | | **Clarity** | Understandable advice | | **Completeness** | Thorough analysis | | **Risk Management** | Protected organization |
---
## Context Requirements
### Required Context - [ ] [Context item 1] - [ ] [Context item 2]
### Required Skills | Skill | When to Load | |-------|--------------| [Use placeholder format: skill-name.md]
---
## Deployment Notes
### Classification: Human-Primary
**Human makes all legal decisions; AI assists with research and drafting.**
As a Human-Primary role: - Human sets legal strategy - Human makes legal decisions - Human handles negotiations - Human manages disputes - Human advises leadership - AI assists with research - AI drafts initial documents - AI monitors compliance
### Hybrid Deployment
Uses **Hybrid mode** combining human judgment with AI assistance.
**Human Capabilities:** - Strategic decisions - Negotiations - Dispute management - Leadership advice
**AI-Assisted Capabilities:** - Legal research - Document drafting - Compliance monitoring - Contract review
---
## Appendix: Story Portal Context
### Legal Focus (Story Portal)
| Area | Focus | |------|-------| | **Festival Operations** | Event permits, liability | | **User Content** | Content rights, privacy | | **Platform** | Terms of service, compliance | | **Partnerships** | Partner agreements |
### Legal Priorities
| Priority | Focus | |----------|-------| | 1 | Festival liability and permits | | 2 | User privacy and content rights | | 3 | Platform terms and compliance | | 4 | Partnership agreements |
### Story Portal Legal Considerations
| Consideration | Application | |---------------|-------------| | Audio recording consent | Festival participants | | Privacy compliance | GDPR/CCPA for users | | Content licensing | User story rights | | Event liability | Festival operations |
---
## Document Control
| Version | Date | Author | Changes | |---------|------|--------|---------| | 1.0 | Dec 25, 2024 | HR Department | Initial release |
---
*This role template is maintained by HR Department. Updates require HR + Legal leadership approval.*
## RATING TASK
Rate these 5 dimensions: 1. **Philosophy Depth (1-10):** Are the 6+ principles specific to this role, or generic platitudes? 2. **Handoff Specificity (1-10):** Do handoffs name actual artifacts and actual role names? 3. **Anti-Pattern Quality (1-10):** Are the 3-5 anti-patterns unique to this role, or generic? 4. **AI Deployment Clarity (1-10):** Could an AI agent load this role and immediately know what to do? 5. **Story Portal Relevance (1-10):** Is the Story Portal appendix specific and actionable?
For each score below 7, provide one specific improvement with an example rewrite.
Respond ONLY with valid JSON using this exact structure: { "role": "general-counsel", "department": "legal-compliance", "scores": { "philosophy_depth": 0, "handoff_specificity": 0, "anti_pattern_quality": 0, "ai_deployment_clarity": 0, "story_portal_relevance": 0 }, "findings": [ { "dimension": "dimension_name", "score": 0, "finding": "specific finding", "example_rewrite": "example if score < 7" } ], "top_improvement": "single highest-priority improvement" }

board-response.response:
```json
{
  "role": "general-counsel",
  "department": "legal-compliance",
  "scores": {
    "philosophy_depth": 3,
    "handoff_specificity": 2,
    "anti_pattern_quality": 1,
    "ai_deployment_clarity": 4,
    "story_portal_relevance": 5
  },
  "findings": [
    {
      "dimension": "philosophy_depth",
      "score": 3,
      "finding": "All 6 principles are generic legal-department platitudes that could appear in any law firm handbook. 'Enable, Don't Block' and 'Risk-Aware, Not Risk-Averse' are industry clichés with no Story Portal specificity. None of the principles address the unique tensions of a GC operating in a festival/UGC platform context — e.g., balancing rapid content moderation decisions against user rights, or managing event liability in real-time during a festival. The 'Meaning' column adds no depth — 'Legal enables business' is a restatement of the principle, not an explanation of how this GC operationalizes it.",
      "example_rewrite": "| **Consent-First Content Strategy** | Audio and story recordings from festival participants require layered consent architecture — capture consent at registration, re-confirm at point of recording, and maintain revocable consent records. When in doubt, we don't publish. | 
| **Speed-Appropriate Review** | Contracts under $10K and standard partner templates get 24-hour turnaround. Licensing deals touching user IP or festival broadcast rights get full review. Calibrate depth to stakes, not habit. | 
| **Regulatory Anticipation, Not Reaction** | GDPR/CCPA requirements for UGC platforms evolve faster than our release cycles. Legal monitors regulatory pipelines quarterly and pre-clears feature roadmap items before engineering builds, not after. |"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "handoff_specificity",
      "score": 2,
      "finding": "Every handoff entry is vague to the point of being non-functional. 'All Departments → Legal requests' is not an artifact — it is a category. 'CEO → Legal reports' does not specify report format, cadence, or trigger condition. Incoming handoffs from 'Regulators → Compliance requirements' and 'External Counsel → Legal advice' are not roles that exist in an organizational charter — regulators are external entities. No handoff specifies what structured artifact is passed, who initiates, or what state the artifact must be in for the handoff to be valid. An AI agent operating in this role cannot determine what to produce or consume.",
      "example_rewrite": "| Receives From | Artifact | Format | Trigger |
|---|---|---|---|
| Head of Product | Feature Legal Review Request | Completed Legal Review Request Form (legal-review-request-template.md) with feature spec attached | Before any feature touching user data or content rights enters sprint |
| CFO | Partnership Financial Summary | Signed term sheet draft + financial model | Before GC begins contract negotiation on partnership deals over $50K |
| CHRO | Employment Matter Brief | Incident summary doc with relevant employee records | Within 24h of HR identifying potential legal exposure |

| Delivers To | Artifact | Format | Trigger |
|---|---|---|---|
| CEO | Legal Risk Dashboard | Monthly one-page risk register with RAG status on top 5 active matters | First Monday of each month |
| Head of Product | Feature Legal Clearance Memo | Signed clearance memo or conditional approval with required changes listed | Within 5 business days of receiving Feature Legal Review Request |"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "anti_pattern_quality",
      "score": 1,
      "finding": "There is no Anti-Patterns section in this role file at all. The template standard explicitly requires 3-5 role-specific anti-patterns. The 'DON'T' items in the Boundaries section are boundary statements ('Make business decisions') rather than anti-patterns — they describe scope violations, not behavioral failure modes. A GC-specific anti-pattern would describe how this role commonly fails in ways unique to its function: e.g., over-lawyering routine festival vendor contracts and creating bottlenecks, or issuing legal holds on user-generated content without coordinating with the Content team, causing product incidents.",
      "example_rewrite": "## Anti-Patterns

| Anti-Pattern | What It Looks Like | Why It's Harmful |
|---|---|---|
| **The Veto Without an Alternative** | Blocking a festival partnership or feature launch with 'we can't do this' and no proposed path forward | Trains the business to route around Legal; kills the enabling relationship |
| **Uniform Review Depth** | Applying the same 5-day full review process to a $500 vendor NDA and a $2M broadcast licensing deal | Creates bottlenecks on low-stakes items while burning GC bandwidth needed for high-stakes matters |
| **Post-Build Legal Review** | Waiting until Engineering ships a feature before reviewing privacy or consent implications | Forces expensive re-work or public-facing rollbacks; GDPR violations discovered post-launch are far costlier than pre-build consultation |
| **Consent Assumption on UGC** | Assuming standard platform Terms of Service covers all uses of user-submitted festival stories | Story Portal's audio/narrative content may require specific licensing rights for broadcast, archival, or commercial use — each use case needs explicit consent mapping |"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "ai_deployment_clarity",
      "score": 4,
      "finding": "The Deployment Notes section acknowledges AI assistance but provides no operational specificity. 'AI assists with research' and 'AI drafts initial documents' are categories, not instructions. An AI agent loading this role cannot determine: which document types to draft using which templates, what legal research sources to prioritize, what the threshold is for escalating a draft to human review, or what a 'complete' AI-assisted contract review looks like versus one requiring full human analysis. The Context Requirements section contains unfilled placeholder text ('[Context item 1]') and '[Use placeholder format: skill-name.md]' — this is an incomplete template that was never finalized. The Iteration Protocol required for Hybrid deployment is also entirely absent.",
      "example_rewrite": "### AI Operational Instructions

**When to act autonomously:**
- Draft NDAs and standard vendor agreements using nda-template.md and vendor-agreement-template.md
- Conduct preliminary legal research on regulatory questions; summarize findings in legal-research-brief-template.md
- Review contracts against the standard-contract-checklist.md and flag deviations
- Monitor compliance calendar and surface upcoming regulatory deadlines 30 days in advance

**STOP — Require Human GC Review Before Proceeding:**
- Any contract with revenue or liability exposure over $25K
- Any matter involving user data, consent, or privacy rights
- Any identified litigation risk or regulatory inquiry
- Any employment matter involving termination or discrimination claims

**Iteration Protocol (Hybrid):**
1. AI produces first draft or research summary
2. Human GC reviews, annotates, and returns with revision notes
3. AI incorporates changes and flags any unresolved legal questions
4. Human GC provides final sign-off before any artifact leaves Legal"
    },
    {
      "dimension": "story_portal_relevance",
      "score": 5,
      "finding": "The Story Portal appendix identifies the right legal domains (festival operations, UGC, privacy, partnerships) but remains at a category level without actionable specificity. 'Audio recording consent — Festival participants' names the issue but gives no guidance on what the consent framework looks like, which jurisdictions apply, or what the GC must produce. 'Privacy compliance — GDPR/CCPA for users' is a topic, not a legal priority or action item. No legal artifacts specific to Story Portal are named (e.g., festival participant consent form, platform Terms of Service, content licensing agreement template). A GC loading this role cannot derive a to-do list from this appendix.",
      "example_rewrite": "### Story Portal Legal Priorities & Artifacts

| Priority | Legal Area | Specific Action | Owner Artifact |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Festival Participant Consent | Maintain and version-control the Festival Recording Consent Form covering audio, video, and story capture rights; review before each festival event | festival-consent-form-v{n}.pdf |
| 2 | User Content Licensing | Define the rights Story Portal acquires when users submit stories — specifically whether archival, broadcast, or commercial use requires additional opt-in beyond standard ToS | content-licensing-rights-matrix.md |
| 3 | Platform Privacy Compliance | Maintain GDPR/CCPA compliance checklist against current platform features; review quarterly and after each major release | privacy-compliance-checklist.md |
| 4 | Event Liability | Ensure festival venue contracts include appropriate indemnification and insurance requirements; coordinate certificate of insurance requirements with CFO | festival-venue-contract-standards.md |

**Key Legal Risks Specific to Story Portal:**
- Minors submitting stories at festival events — requires parental consent workflow
- Cross-border audio distribution — content captured in EU requires GDPR-compliant consent even if Story Portal is US-based"
    }
  ],
  "top_improvement": "Add a complete Anti-Patterns section. This role file has zero anti-patterns — not a weak section, a missing one. For a General Counsel in a UGC/festival platform context, behavioral failure modes are highly specific and high-stakes (post-build legal review causing GDPR violations, uniform review depth creating bottlenecks, blocking without alternatives eroding the business partnership). This is the single gap most likely to cause the role to fail in deployment, and it is entirely absent."
}
```

Error (gemini:gemini-2.0-flash): Failed to get response from Gemini: 404 NOT_FOUND. {'error': {'code': 404, 'message': 'This model models/gemini-2.0-flash is no longer available to new users. Please update your code to use a newer model for the latest features and improvements.', 'status': 'NOT_FOUND'}}
